items.title: A Mayor Is Taking Maternity Leave in Japan. Some Men Are Furious.
items.summary: Shoko Kawata is the first mayor to take maternity leave in Japan’s history. Her decision has prompted debate about gender discrimination in the work force.

items.title: Drones Used to Fight Mosquitoes as Dengue Fever Surges in Sri Lanka
items.summary: The military has joined the battle against the insects, as an outbreak strains hospitals in a country already reeling from energy shortages caused by the Iran War.

items.title: ‘Every Year After’ Renewed for Season 2 at Amazon; Will Focus on Charlie’s Story From ‘One Golden Summer’ Sequel Book
items.summary: Get ready to return to Barry’s Bay: “Every Year After” has been renewed for a second season at Amazon’s Prime Video less than a month after its debut. Based on Carley Fortune’s bestselling novel “Every Summer After,” the first season of the drama tells the friends-to-lovers-to-second-chance-romance story of Percy Fraser (Sadie Soverall) and Sam Florek […]

Douglas Stuart: “From the age of 12 to just about 16, I had to be part of a Protestant gang and fight Catholics"
items.summary: You’d be a complete fannybaws not to love <i>John Of John</i>, the latest novel from Douglas Stuart which might just win him a second Booker Prize. Forbidden love, bible-bashing, knitted erections, acid house, The Smiths, Oprah Winfrey, Dua Lipa and CMAT are all discussed as he meets Stuart Clark.

items.title: Screen time can damage under-twos’ development, landmark study suggests
